{{Quote|Look at all that new construction. They aren't kidding around, are they?|[[Dankin]], to [[Talon Karrde]]|<ref name="TLC" />}}
The '''Bilbringi Shipyards''' were a major military [[Starship/Legends|starship]]-production facility, first controlled by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], then later the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], located in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] with the [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]] [[Bilbringi VII]], in the [[Bilbringi system/Legends|Bilbringi system]].

==Description==
{{Quote|Many of the asteroids were being mined for use in the shipyards, so Imperial forces were deployed not only in the shipyards but close to many of the extraction operations. Even with prior authorization, it was difficult to navigate through the system because of all the checkpoints.|[[Tobb Jadak]]|<ref name="MF">[[Millennium Falcon (novel)|''Millennium Falcon'']]</ref>}}
The Bilbringi Shipyards were orbital platforms that circled [[Bilbringi VII]], a medium sized [[Planetoid/Legends|planetoid]] located in the life-devoid [[Bilbringi system/Legends|Bilbringi system]],<ref name="TLCS" /> in the [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]].<ref name="EAOC" /> The [[Shipyard/Legends|shipyards]] had been built in this system because of its proximity to Imperial convoy routes and the preponderance of available metals and ores.<ref name="TLCS" /> The [[Bilbringi asteroid fields]] that surrounded the shipyards were [[Mining/Legends|mined]] for their resources but made the travel to the facilities very complicated.<ref name="MF" />

The shipyards formed a large and efficient ship-building facility dedicated to the production of warships. The orbital platforms were used for virtually every stage of starship construction. They included open dry-docks for the overhaul of hyperdrive engines, heavy lifting platforms, a work area for the construction of space vessel superstructures, and a firing platform to fine-tune weaponry.<ref name="TLCS" /> Although they were not as imposing as the [[CEC shipyards|Corellia]], [[Fondor Shipyards/Legends|Fondor]], [[Kuat Drive Yards Orbital Array|Kuat]] shipyards, the Bilbringi Shipyards encompassed thousands of dry docks and half completed warships.<ref name="TEGtPaM" />

==History==
{{Quote|The Imperial shipyards at Bilbringi. And I know what you're all saying to yourselves: it's big, it's well defended, and what in the galaxy is the high command thinking about? The answer is simple: it's big, it's well defended, and it's the last place the Imperials will expect us to hit.|[[Crix Madine/Legends|Crix Madine]]|<ref name="TLC" />}}
The construction of the Bilbringi Shipyards was an idea originally conceived by [[Count of Serenno/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]].<ref name="TCW:SM4">''[[The Clone Wars: Secret Missions 4: Guardians of the Chiss Key]]''</ref> At that time, the [[Bilbringi Depot]] on Bilbringi VII was the primary settlement in the system.<ref name="TCW:SM3">''[[The Clone Wars: Secret Missions 3: Duel at Shattered Rock]]''</ref> Dooku intended to acquire the Depot from [[Drixo]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] to transform it into a shipyard for the [[Confederate Navy/Legends|Confederate Navy]]. However, the plot was foiled by the Republic that seized the Bilbringi facility.<ref name="TCW:SM4" />

The Bilbringi system was later developed as a starship construction facility and testing ground for experimental weaponry by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].<ref name="TEGtPaM">''[[The Essential Guide to Planets and Moons]]''</ref> The Bilbringi Shipyards were [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial Military]] shipyards<ref name="TEGtW">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref> exclusively dedicated to the construction of vessels for the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]].<ref name="TLCS" /> During the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial Period]], the shipyards could not produce ships larger than [[Cruiser/Legends|cruisers]].<ref name="TEGtW" /> During the early years of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] attempted, unsuccessfully, to destroy the Bilbringi Shipyards using a [[baradium fission device]] shipped to the yards via the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']].<ref name="MF" />

[[File:Bilbringi.jpg|thumb|left|260px|Thrawn's fleet arrives at the shipyards to [[Battle of Bilbringi (9 ABY)|ambush]] New Republic forces]]
After the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], the Bilbringi and its shipyards remained a major component of the Imperial war machine. During the [[Thrawn campaign]] in [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]], the Bilbringi facility were the largest shipyard controlled by [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]]. At that time, the Bilbringi Shipyards had been upgraded to be able to produce [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyers]]. The facility was under the command of [[General/Legends|General]] [[Theol Drost|Drost]] and highly defended.<ref name="TLCS" /> The defense system included four [[Golan II Space Defense SpaceGun|Golan II platforms]] and [[Deflector shield generator/Legends|shield generators]].<ref name="TEGtPaM" /> However, the very size of the shipyards made them vulnerable to attack.<ref name="TLCS" /> In retaliation for the Imperial [[Ambush on Trogan|attack]] on the [[Smugglers' Alliance]] meeting on [[Trogan/Legends|Trogan]], smugglers [[Raid on Bilbringi|raided]] the yards and destroyed an [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]] under construction. During the raid, [[Talon Karrde]] and his crew found at that the Empire was working on a group of asteroids which were being outfitted with cloaking devices. Karrde would later give the data to the New Republic after Thrawn used the asteroids to lay [[Siege of Coruscant|siege]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]].<ref name="TLCS" /> Later the same year, the Bilbringi Shipyards were the site of the [[Battle of Bilbringi (9 ABY)|showdown]] between Thrawn's forces and the New Republic – though the Republic took heavy losses, in the end they were victorious.<ref name="TLC" /> The shipyards were heavily damaged during the battle and eventually abandoned by the Empire.<ref name="TLCS" />

The damaged Bilbringi Shipyards were later seized by the New Republic, which repaired the stations and retrofitted the Lusankya there. The [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|''Executor''-class Star Dreadnought]] ''[[Lusankya/Legends|Lusankya]]'', captured by the New Republic, was later moved to the Bilbringi yards to be retrofitted.<ref name="TCSWE">{{CSWECite|I|73|Bilbringi}}</ref> After the end of the [[Hegemony theater]], the former [[Director of Imperial Intelligence]] [[Ysanne Isard]] [[Mission to Bilbringi|infiltrated]] the shipyards in an unsuccessful attempt to recapture the ''Lusankya'' for the Empire.<ref name="XW:IR" /> Circa [[12 ABY/Legends|12 ABY]], the rogue [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] student [[Dal Konur]] was confronted in the [[Hangar Bay 631-D]] of the shipyards by a group of agents sent by [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]].<ref name="RJ" />

During the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]], the Bilbringi Shipyards were under heavy demands to build vessels for the New Republic against the [[Yuuzhan Vong]]. When [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]] visited the shipyards in [[27 ABY/Legends|27 ABY]] in the hopes of convincing General [[Muun (individual)|Muun]] to aid in the defense of [[Vray]] refugees, the shipyards were constructing some five hundred vessels and two ''Imperial''-class Star Destroyers were near completion.<ref name="SbS" /> The shipyards were defended by the [[Bilbringi Defense Force]].<ref name="TFP" /> Five [[Cruiser/Legends|cruisers]] and fifteen [[Corvette/Legends|corvettes]] constituted a quarter of the defense fleet station there, which also included the [[Dauntless (cruiser)|''Dauntless'']].<ref name="SbS" /> Nevertheless, the yards were [[Attack on the Bilbringi system|taken]] by the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] in 27 ABY,<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> and were mostly destroyed, with [[shipwomb]]s taking the place of mechanical shipbuilding means. The Bilbringi system and its shipyards were later targeted by the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]] during [[Second Battle of Bilbringi|Operation Trinity]] but the mission failed.<ref name="TFP" />

[[40 ABY/Legends|Ten years after]] the Yuuzhan Vong surrendered, Jacen Solo as Darth Caedus used the Shipyards as a bargaining chip, along with [[Borleias/Legends|Borleias]], for the Imperial Remnant to join the Confederation War.<ref name="Revelation" /> While Borleias would revert to Galactic Alliance control after that war, Bilbringi remained with the Imperial Remnant.<ref name="Conviction">''[[Fate of the Jedi: Conviction]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
The PC game [[Star Wars: Rebellion (video game)|''Star Wars: Rebellion'']] names the Bilbringi Shipyards as the "Imperial Shipyards," claiming that the bulk of the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] was crafted there. [[Kuat Drive Yards/Legends|Kuat Drive Yards]], in fact, holds this distinction.
